name: Route of the Vía Verde de la Campiña - round trip from Écija
name_original: Ruta de la Vía Verde de la Campiña — ida y vuelta desde Écija
region_intro: Hiking around Écija, located in the heart of Andalusia, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The region is characterized by undulating fields, agricultural landscapes, and the fluvial terraces of the Genil River. Notable features include the Vía Verde de la Campiña, which follows an old railway line, and the Santa Lucía Valley with its orange groves. This countryside provides varied terrain for hikers. Best hiking trails around Écija The most popular hiking route is Carolinas Fountains Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through extensive orange groves and agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Vía Verde de la Campiña - round trip from Écija, a moderate 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This trail follows an old railway line, offering accessible and scenic views of the Écija countryside. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the Virgen de la Torre — circular from La Carlota, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through Andalusian landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Écija is defined by greenways, riverine environments, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Écija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 47 reviews. More than 141 hikers have used komoot to explore Écija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Écija?
A: Écija offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking routes, catering to various fitness levels. These trails explore the region's undulating fields, agricultural landscapes, and riverine environments.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Écija?
A: Hiking in Écija will immerse you in a landscape characterized by extensive agricultural fields, especially during spring and summer when citrus blossoms are in bloom in areas like the Santa Lucía Valley. You'll also encounter the fluvial terraces of the Genil River and the scenic countryside of La Campiña, often with picturesque views of Écija's historic towers.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Écija?
A: Yes, Écija has several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248228530/carolinas-fountains-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carolinas Fountains Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.5 km path that leads through beautiful orange groves and agricultural landscapes, perfect for a leisurely stroll.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Écija?
A: Many trails in Écija are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those in open countryside like the Vía Verde de la Campiña. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific routes or natural parks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Écija area?
A: Yes, Écija offers several circular routes. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243732412/ascent-to-the-virgen-de-la-torre-circular-from-la-carlota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Virgen de la Torre — circular from La Carlota&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through classic Andalusian landscapes. For a more demanding option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996313658/la-carlota-and-la-guijarrosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carlota and La Guijarrosa Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; provides a challenging experience with historical landmarks.

Q: What is the Vía Verde de la Campiña like for hiking?
A: The Vía Verde de la Campiña is a highlight for hikers and cyclists. This 'Green Path' follows an old railway line, offering an accessible and scenic trail through the Écija countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010056962/route-of-the-via-verde-de-la-campina-round-trip-from-ecija" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Vía Verde de la Campiña - round trip from Écija&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option to experience this unique path.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Écija is known as the 'City of Towers.' You can explore historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6429603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España (El Salón)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Santa María and Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Écija countryside&lt;/a&gt; itself offers stunning viewpoints, and you might even spot distant castles like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4488840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt; from certain vantage points.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Écija?
A: The best time to hike in Écija is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers comfortable temperatures and beautiful colors. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late evenings preferable for hiking.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Écija?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996313658/la-carlota-and-la-guijarrosa-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carlota and La Guijarrosa Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult. This 20 km trail presents more challenging terrain and offers a rewarding exploration of the Andalusian landscapes and historical landmarks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Écija?
A: The hiking routes in Écija are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 47 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from easy strolls through orange groves to more challenging routes with scenic views of the countryside and historic sites.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Écija?
A: While Écija itself is accessible by bus from larger cities like Seville, direct public transport to specific rural trailheads can be limited. It's often best to plan for personal vehicle access or consider local taxi services to reach the starting points of some of the more remote routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Écija?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Écija, particularly near the town center for routes that start there. For trails like the Vía Verde de la Campiña, there are often designated parking areas at access points along the route, though specific availability can vary. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for parking suggestions near your chosen trailhead.
tips: Écija is a municipality of Seville but nevertheless, this municipality is closer to the province of Córdoba than to the provincial capital, Seville. The city popularly known as Ciudad del Sol, the City of Towers and the Pan of Andalusia (because of its high temperatures, especially during the summer seasons). It is considered one of the most important Historical Sites in Andalusia.

The Vía Verde de la Campiña is reformed in the area of Écija, approximately 30 kilometers from the same town to the city of Córdoba. The path is surrounded by agricultural fields located on hills forming impressive views of the area!
